nihafs-macbook-air.always_on: yes (per owner — both home and office machines stay powered 24/7 on same WiFi)
nihafs-macbook-air.purpose: Claude Code dev workstation; mobile (still primary brain even when not at a fixed network).
nihafs-macbook-air.notes: Tailscale auto-named from macOS Computer Name ("Nihaf's MacBook Air"). Network hostname is still Mac.lan — they're different fields in macOS. Acceptable as-is; rename via login.tailscale.com/admin if a cleaner identifier is wanted later.

hn-winpc.primary_user_note: Username has a space. OpenSSH ssh_config doesn't parse spaces in User field, so connect with: ssh "HN Hotels@hn-winpc" (quoted on command line). Long-term cleanup: create a no-space Windows user via SSH then update authorized_keys for that user.
hn-winpc.purpose: PRIMARY appliance — aggregator pulse runs here (Chrome + extension 24/7). Long-term home for any Windows-bound automation. Designated primary because the hardware is purpose-built for 24/7 mini-PC business use, modern Chrome, and clean storage.

hardening.power: powercfg standby-timeout-ac/dc=0, hibernate=off (file removed), monitor=30min only

hn-winpc.notes: HP EliteDesk DM is a 35W mini-PC built for always-on business use — SSD, no moving parts beyond a small fan, gigabit ethernet. To be onboarded via fleet/setup-winpc.ps1. Replace null fields after registration. Win10 EOL was Oct 2025 but as appliance behind Tailscale this is acceptable for 1-2 years; long-term move is Linux on this hardware.

hn-imac.chip: 3.4 GHz quad-core Intel Core i5 (Kaby Lake, i5-7500)
hn-imac.max_supported_macos_note: 2017 5K iMac is on Sonoma's compatibility list. Once storage is freed (need 25GB+ free for safety), can update Mojave 10.14 → Big Sur → Monterey → Ventura → Sonoma in steps OR jump to Sonoma directly via Apple's Software Update.
hn-imac.purpose: SECONDARY appliance — backup macOS automation host, also useful for any macOS-only tooling. Originally planned as primary but Windows PC has better hardware/storage/Chrome/network for the aggregator role.
hn-imac.notes: Storage full → blocks macOS update. Once SSH bridge is up, I clean storage remotely from laptop. Tailscale on Mojave needs the LEGACY pkg from pkgs.tailscale.com/stable (latest GUI version requires macOS 13+). The setup-imac.sh script will detect Mojave and skip brew.
